mission.title: kubeflow: ambassador and vizier-core not starting
mission.description: ambassador and vizier-core not starting. This issue affects 4+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
I am using current master (commit 58022fb) and installed kubeflow on a previously created AWS EKS cluster (thus not using `apply platform` provided by kubeflow).

steps.title: Apply the fix for ambassador and vizier-core not starting
steps.description: As discussed in #3259 I changed the namespace label in the AWS setup for the kubeflow namespace, so that istio injection is by-default disabled. --- This change is [

steps.title: Confirm ambassador and vizier-core not starting is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: As discussed in #3259 I changed the namespace label in the AWS setup for the kubeflow namespace, so that istio injection is by-default disabled. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/3289)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
